content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1109 K Street
Sacramento 95814
(916) 322-5660                                                February 4, 1976

A-76-33L

Don Kinchlos, Treasurer
Operating Engineers Local Union #3
474 Valencia Street
San Francisco CA 94103

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 76-011

Dear Mr. Kinchlos:

Thank you for your letter of January 19, 1976, requesting an opinion with respect to your organization's reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act. Since your opinion request does not raise a substantial question of interpretation under the Act, no formal opinion will be issued. I hope, however, that the following informal comments will be helpful.

The advice you received from Ms. Kutchins with respect to the proper method of reporting was correct. Each campaign statement that you file with respect to a particular election should disclose all expenditures and contributions during the reporting period and not only those related to the particular election in which your organization is involved. See California Government Code Section 84210. The staff recognizes, however, that this method of reporting does create certain problems and presently is considering proposing legislative amendments designed to rectify the problems.

With respect to the statements you already have filed, I would suggest that you adopt the following procedure. You should file your semi-annual statement reporting those expenditures and contributions which have not previously been disclosed through December 31, 1975. You should then send a copy of this semi-annual campaign statement to the various filing officers with whom you previously filed campaign statements relating to special elections and request that the semi-annual statement be attached to your original campaign statement as a supplement. You also should indicate to your filing officer that you are following this procedure pursuant to the instructions of the Commission. In the future, of course, you should file complete campaign statements for every election unless, and until, the Act is amended.

Don Kinchloe
February 4, 1976
Page Two


     Thank you for your interest in the Political Reform
Act. If you have any further questions, please contact Kenneth
Pinney, Chief of the Commission's Legal Division.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                        Chairman, for the
                                        Commission

DHL:pvp

Date Received 1/29/76
Received 2-5-76

Operating Engineers Local Union No. 3
OF THE
INTERNATIONAL UNION OF OPERATING ENGINEERS
AFFILIATED WITH THE AFL-CIO

Jurisdiction
Northern California, Northern Nevada,
Utah, Hawaii and Mid-Pacific Islands

Telephone 431-1365
474 Valencia Street
San Francisco, California 94103

January 19, 1976

Mr. Dan Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

Exercising the right under California Administrative Code, Title 2,
Administration, Division 6 Fair Political Practices Commission, 18320,
as treasurer of Operating Engineers Local Union No. 3, I.U. 274-3063 we
are hereby requesting an opinion of the Fair Political Practices Commission.

Operating Engineers Local Union No. 3 is a major donor supporting
candidates and ballot measures, and officeholders in the State of California. A statement of organization was filed with the Secretary of State's
office on April 5, 1974, under the Waxman-Dymally law, and filed again
under Proposition 9 law on February 3, 1975. All contributions received
by Operating Engineers Local Union No. 3 never amount to more than $50
from any one single source.

After the inception of Proposition 9, Operating Engineers has complied
with campaign reporting on all special elections, concerning assembly
races, senatorial races, city and county elections by following each
individual reporting requirements of the respective reporting offices.
A list of all reports made by Operating Engineers Local Union No. 3 in
1975 under Proposition 9 follows.

Period Covered      Date Filed    Filing Officer            Election

2/23/75-3/22/75     3/26/75      Secretary of State,       29th Senatorial
                                 Los Angeles County,       District, 15th
                                 Alameda County            Assembly District

Mr. Dan Lowenstein                -2-                January 19, 1976


Period Covered      Date Filed    Filing Officer           Election

1/7/75-3/31/75      4/8/75       Secretary of State,      All activity
                                  Marin County,            between 1/7-3/31/75
                                  San Rafael City Clerk,   (filed as a pre-
                                  Sutter County, Yuba      caution, at this
                                  City Clerk, Tuolumne     time, definite
                                  County, Alameda County,  reporting periods
                                  San Francisco Regis-     not set up by
                                  trar, Los Angeles        F.P.P.C.)
                                  County, Fresno County,
                                  Santa Clara County,
                                  Stanislaus County,
                                  Modesto City Clerk

3/11/75-4/5/75      4/8/75       Oakland City Clerk,      Oakland City
                                  Alameda County

1/7/75-3/31/75      4/24/75      Modesto City Clerk,      Amended statement
                                  Stanislaus County

3/29/75-5/3/75      5/6/75       Richmond City Clerk      Richmond City

4/6/75-5/3/75       5/6/75       Oakland City Clerk       Oakland City

3/23/75-5/30/75     6/5/75       Los Angeles County       29th Senatorial
                                                           District

5/4/75-6/12/75      6/17/75      Oakland City Clerk       Oakland City

5/4/75-7/10/75      6/17/75      Richmond City Clerk      Richmond City

1/7/75-6/30/75      7/31/75      Secretary of State,      Off-year statement,
                                  San Francisco County,    semi-annual report
                                  Los Angeles County,
                                  Santa Clara County,
                                  Richmond City Clerk,
                                  Manteca City Clerk,
                                  Modesto City Clerk,
                                  San Rafael City Clerk,
                                  Yuba City Clerk,
                                  Tuolumne County, Alameda
                                  County, Fresno County,
                                  Oakland City Clerk

1/7/75-9/22/75      9/24/75      San Francisco Regis-     San Francisco City
                                  trar

7/1/75-10/28/75     10/29/75     Sacramento City Clerk    Sacramento City

Mr. Dan Lowenstein                    -3-                    January 19, 1976


Period Covered        Date Filed    Filing Officer(s)           Election(s)

9/23/75-10/25/75     10/28/75      Secretary of State,         4th Assembly District
                                   Yolo County, Solano
                                   County, Sacramento
                                   County

10/26/75-11/22/75    11/25/75      Secretary of State,         4th Assembly District
                                   Yolo County, Solano
                                   County, Sacramento
                                   County

9/2/75-9/29/75       10/2/75       Stockton City Clerk         Stockton City

9/23/75-10/22/75     10/23/75      San Francisco Reg-          San Francisco City
                                   istrar

9/30/75-12/11/75     12/18/75      Stockton City Clerk         Stockton City

10/20/75-1/8/76      1/8/76        Alameda County,             Alameda County
                                   Contra Costa County

10/20/75-1/8/76      1/8/76        Marin County                Novato City

10/23/75-1/8/76      1/8/76        San Francisco Reg-          San Francisco City
                                   istrar


     In filing the special election reports, each individual city or
county election was handed separately, with separate cumulative totals.
As we understood it, the objective was to show only what activity took place
in each respective area covered by a special election.

     It was our intention to file the January 31, 1976 report to the
Secretary of State, showing all activity between July 1, 1975 through
December 31, 1975 listing everything that was reported on all special
election reports, as well as all other contributions made to officeholders
not currently running for election. Several expenditures were made to
officeholders during the period of July 1, 1975 through December 31, 1975,
that we felt did not belong on any of the special election reports - but
belonged on the off-year statement due on January 31, 1976 to the Secretary
of State.

Mr. Dan Lowenstein                -4-                    January 19, 1976


     After talking with Regina Kutchins of the Fair Political Practices
Commission, we discovered that our method of reporting is incorrect.
We learned that the correct way is to report all expenditures we made
during each special reporting period. All expenditures were supposed
to be listed, even though they may not have pertained to that special
city or county in which we were reporting.

     Example:  On September 11, 1975 we issued a check to Lt. Governor
                Dymally Northern Account in the amount of $500. Should
                this contribution have been reported to the San Francisco
                Registrar during the period of 1/7/75-9/22/75?

     Example:  On November 10, 1975 we issued a check to the Committee
                to Re-Elect Senator Randolph Collier in the amount of
                $1,000. Should this have been reported to the Stockton
                City Clerk during the period of 9/30/75-12/11/75?

     If the above is correct, we will have several problems. First,
are we expected to report expenditures that we make to a statewide or other
officeholder on every special election report? Second, do we ever have
to notify the Secretary of State, and any other reporting officers, of
expenditures to statewide or other officeholders?

     It was our understanding that we are only supposed to report
expenditures for statewide or other officeholders to the Secretary of
State, and other respective reporting officers, on off-year campaign
statements, and that there was no necessity to report such expenditures
on special election reports.

     It is now our understanding that we should report such expenditures
only on special election reports and not to the Secretary of State. This
seems to vitiate the purpose of the reports, since the Secretary of State
would never know of such contributions.

     Since we have not reported expenditures for statewide and other
officeholders on special election reports, but only on off-year statements,
through June 30, 1975, what should we do now to comply with the law?
It would be our preference to send the off-year report through
December 31, 1975, listing all expenditures made, and to hereafter report
such expenditures as you decide.

Mr. Dan Lowenstein                    -5-                    January 19, 1976


Thank you for your anticipated advise.

                                        Sincerely yours,

                                        OPERATING ENGINEERS LOCAL UNION NO. 3

                                        [signature]
                                        DON KINCHLOE
                                        TREASURER

DK/jma
